title: Welding Cost Calculator
description: Industrial-grade calculator for estimating total welding cost per weld, incorporating labor, consumables, energy, overhead, and quality loss drivers. Aligned with ISO 3834, AWS D1.1, and Lean Six Sigma principles.

inputs.label: Weld Length (mm)
inputs.businessContext: Total length of the weld joint to be deposited.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total length of the weld joint to be deposited.

inputs.label: Weld Throat Thickness (mm)
inputs.businessContext: Effective throat thickness of the fillet or groove weld.

businessContext_i18n.en: Effective throat thickness of the fillet or groove weld.

inputs.label: Material Density (g/cm³)
inputs.businessContext: Density of the base and filler material (typical steel ~7.85).

businessContext_i18n.en: Density of the base and filler material (typical steel ~7.85).

inputs.label: Deposition Efficiency (%)
inputs.businessContext: Ratio of deposited weld metal to total filler metal consumed (includes spatter and losses).

businessContext_i18n.en: Ratio of deposited weld metal to total filler metal consumed (includes spatter and losses).

inputs.label: Labor Rate (USD/hour)
inputs.businessContext: Fully burdened hourly labor cost for welder.

businessContext_i18n.en: Fully burdened hourly labor cost for welder.

inputs.label: Welding Speed (mm/min)
inputs.businessContext: Travel speed of the welding torch along the joint.

businessContext_i18n.en: Travel speed of the welding torch along the joint.

inputs.label: Filler Wire Cost (USD/kg)
inputs.businessContext: Cost per kilogram of filler wire or electrode.

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost per kilogram of filler wire or electrode.

inputs.label: Shielding Gas Cost (USD/L)
inputs.businessContext: Cost per liter of shielding gas (e.g., Argon/CO2 mix).

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost per liter of shielding gas (e.g., Argon/CO2 mix).

inputs.label: Gas Flow Rate (L/min)
inputs.businessContext: Flow rate of shielding gas during welding.

businessContext_i18n.en: Flow rate of shielding gas during welding.

inputs.label: Welding Power (kW)
inputs.businessContext: Average power consumption of the welding machine during operation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average power consumption of the welding machine during operation.

inputs.label: Overhead Rate (USD/hour)
inputs.businessContext: Facility and equipment overhead allocated per hour of welding.

businessContext_i18n.en: Facility and equipment overhead allocated per hour of welding.

inputs.label: Rework Rate (%)
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of welds requiring rework (defect rate).

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of welds requiring rework (defect rate).

inputs.label: Rework Cost Factor
inputs.businessContext: Multiplier on original cost to account for rework (cutting, re-welding, inspection).

businessContext_i18n.en: Multiplier on original cost to account for rework (cutting, re-welding, inspection).

inputs.label: Welding Process Type
inputs.businessContext: Standard arc welding process per AWS A3.0.

businessContext_i18n.en: Standard arc welding process per AWS A3.0.

inputs.label: Use Premium Filler Material
inputs.businessContext: Toggle to apply a 20% cost premium for high-performance filler wire.

businessContext_i18n.en: Toggle to apply a 20% cost premium for high-performance filler wire.

rules.message: Weld length and throat thickness must be positive.
Deposition efficiency must be between 50% and 100%.
Rework rate must be between 0% and 50%.
Gas flow rate must be between 5 and 50 L/min.

thresholds.message: Labor rate exceeds $80/h. Consider process automation or skill mix optimization.
Deposition efficiency below 70%. Investigate spatter, wire feed issues, or parameter settings.
Rework rate above 10%. Implement Six Sigma DMAIC to reduce defects.
Energy cost per kWh exceeds $0.20. Consider energy-efficient welding equipment.

formulas.name: Weld Volume (cm³)
formulas.description: Volume of deposited weld metal assuming a triangular fillet cross-section.

formulas.name: Filler Mass Required (kg)
formulas.description: Mass of filler metal needed accounting for deposition efficiency.

formulas.name: Labor Time (hours)
formulas.description: Total arc-on time for the weld.

formulas.name: Filler Material Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Cost of filler wire including premium if selected.

formulas.name: Shielding Gas Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Cost of shielding gas consumed during welding.

formulas.name: Energy Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Cost of electrical energy for welding.

formulas.name: Overhead Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Allocated overhead for the welding operation.

formulas.name: Rework Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Expected cost of rework based on defect rate.

formulas.name: Total Welding Cost (USD)
formulas.description: Primary result: total cost per weld including rework.
formulas.formula: filler_cost + gas_cost + energy_cost + labor_cost + overhead_cost + rework_cost

primary.label: Total Welding Cost
primary.description: Total cost per weld joint including all direct and indirect costs.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Spatter Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Filler material lost as spatter, not deposited.
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Increases filler cost by up to 15%.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Defect Scrap
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Cost of scrapped parts due to non-repairable defects.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Consumable Wear
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Cost of contact tips, nozzles, liners.

suggestedActions.description: Increase welding speed or reduce power to lower energy and labor costs.
Implement statistical process control (SPC) to reduce rework rate below 5%.
Switch to low-spatter wire or optimize shielding gas mix.
Evaluate robotic welding for high-volume joints to reduce labor cost.
